perturbation.original_text: Institution agrees to u se the Confidential Information only for evaluation purposes to determine whether to conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser . Institution shall not use the Confidential Information for its own b enefit or the benefit of another. and the Regents of the University of Michigan (“Institution”) having business offices at 3003 S. State St., 1000 Wolverine Tower, Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1274.
perturbation.changed_text: Institution agrees to use the Confidential Information only for evaluation purposes to determine whether to conduct clinical studies. Institution shall not use the Confidential Information for its own b enefit or the benefit of another. and the Regents of the University of Michigan (“Institution”) having business offices at 3003 S. State St., 1000 Wolverine Tower, Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1274.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "sponsored by Discloser" from the first sentence, the agreement now suggests that Institution can use the information to evaluate any clinical studies, not just those sponsored by the Discloser. This introduces a contradiction because the stated 'Purpose' in Section 1 is specifically limited to studies sponsored by the Discloser.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From time to time during the term of this Agreement, Discloser may disclose to Ins titution certain confidential information concerning Discloser’s clinical studies for the purpose of permitting Institution to evaluate whether to conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser (the Purpose”). A general description of the confidential information to be disclosed is attached as Appendix A.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the Institution's use of Confidential Information to evaluating clinical studies 'sponsored by Discloser'. The changed text removes 'sponsored by Discloser,' broadening the scope to include any clinical studies. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which explicitly states the purpose of the agreement is to evaluate studies 'sponsored by Discloser.' The modified text allows for a broader interpretation of use, conflicting with the defined purpose, thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Institution agrees not to disclose or ot herwise make available any of the Confidential Information to anyone except those employees and agents of Institution who need to know the Confidential Information to carry out the Purpose and who are bound by obligations of non- use and non -disclosure substantially similar to those set forth herein . Institution shall be responsible for any disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by its employees or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: Institution agrees not to disclose or ot herwise make available any of the Confidential Information to anyone except those employees and agents of Institution who need to know the Confidential Information and who are bound by obligations of non- use and non -disclosure substantially similar to those set forth herein . Institution shall be responsible for any disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by its employees or agents.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "to carry out the Purpose" has been removed. This implies that Institution employees can access confidential information regardless of whether it aligns with the defined Purpose stated in Section 1, creating an inconsistency and potential misuse of disclosed information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From time to time during the term of this Agreement, Discloser may disclose to Ins titution certain confidential information concerning Discloser’s clinical studies for the purpose of permitting Institution to evaluate whether to conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser (the Purpose”). A general description of the confidential information to be disclosed is attached as Appendix A.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts access to confidential information to employees and agents who need it 'to carry out the Purpose' defined in Section 1. The changed text removes this purpose restriction. The contradicted text defines 'the Purpose' as evaluating whether Institution will conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser. By removing 'to carry out the Purpose,' the changed text allows Institution employees and agents access to confidential information even if it's unrelated to evaluating clinical studies, directly contradicting the limited scope defined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Institution shall protect Discloser’s Confidential Information using not less than the same care it uses with respect to its own confidential information, but at all times at least reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Institution shall protect Confidential Information using not less than the same care it uses with respect to its own information.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "Discloser’s" and "but at all times at least reasonable care" were removed from this sentence. This means it is not specifically Discloser's confidential information which must be protected, this creates confusion and in-text contradiction as there is no need to keep Discloser's specific Confidential Information secure, and makes the level of care ambiguous, as reasonable care is no longer required at all times.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information” means any information , including investigator’s brochures, case report forms and protocols, disclosed by or on behalf of Disclosure identified as confidential when first disclosed and provided in tangible form, or if disclosed orally summarized in a writing pro vided by the Discloser to Institution within twenty (20) days after oral disclosure, other than information that: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by In stitution; (b) is already known by or in the possessio n of Institution at the time of disclosure by Discloser; (c) is indepen dently developed by In stitution witho ut use of or refere nce to Discl oser’s Confidential Information; or (d) is obtained by Institution from a third party that has not breached any obligations of confident iality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'Discloser’s' from 'Confidential Information', implying that the institution is no longer specifically obligated to protect the discloser's confidential information as explicitly defined in the contradicted text. The removal of 'but at all times at least reasonable care' also makes the level of care ambiguous, contradicting the implicit requirement of reasonable care within the definition of Confidential Information, creating an in-text contradiction according to the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Institution may disclose the Confidential Information to the extent that such disclosure is required by law or court order, provided, however, that Institution promptly provides to Discl oser p rior written not ice of suc h disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Institution may disclose the Confidential Information to the extent that such disclosure is required by law or court order.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "provided, however, that Institution promptly provides to Discloser prior written notice of such disclosure", Institution is no longer obliged to notify Discloser of any disclosure. This directly contradicts the obligation of confidentiality outlined in Section 2, and the purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information” means any information , including investigator’s brochures, case report forms and protocols, disclosed by or on behalf of Disclosure identified as confidential when first disclosed and provided in tangible form, or if disclosed orally summarized in a writing pro vided by the Discloser to Institution within twenty (20) days after oral disclosure, other than information that: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by In stitution; (b) is already known by or in the possessio n of Institution at the time of disclosure by Discloser; (c) is indepen dently developed by In stitution witho ut use of or refere nce to Discl oser’s Confidential Information; or (d) is obtained by Institution from a third party that has not breached any obligations of confident iality.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Institution to notify the Discloser prior to disclosing Confidential Information as required by law or court order. The changed text removes this notification requirement. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the Contradicted Text because without notification, the Discloser is unable to protect its confidential information, potentially leading to public availability of information that should be protected. Thus, the Institution's ability to disclose without notice creates a contradiction in the context of the agreement's purpose of maintaining confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, all Discloser’s Confidential Information related to a clinical study if Institution does not participate in such clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest by th e Discl oser, provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.
perturbation.changed_text: Institution shall promptly return o r, at Discloser’s option, destr oy, all Confidential Information if Institution does not participate in a clinical study, upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, or upon written req uest.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Discloser's' before Confidential Information and removing "provided however, that Institution may retain in confidence under this Agreement one archived copy of the Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the purpose of administering In stitution’s obligations hereun der.", the institution is now oblidged to return or destroy all Confidential Information, including its archived copy, which introduces contradiction, as there is no longer a retained copy of the Confidential Information for administering Institutions obligations as stated in other sections
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Confidentiality 3.1 Use. Institution agrees to u se the Confidential Information only for evaluation purposes to determine whether to conduct clinical studies sponsored by Discloser . Institution shall not use the Confidential Information for its own b enefit or the benefit of another. and the Regents of the University of Michigan (“Institution”) having business offices at 3003 S. State St., 1000 Wolverine Tower, Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1274.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Institution to retain an archived copy of the Discloser's Confidential Information for administering its obligations. The changed text removes this allowance, requiring the Institution to return or destroy *all* Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Institution uses the Confidential Information (implying it possesses it) for evaluation purposes related to clinical studies sponsored by the Discloser. Without the retained copy, the Institution can no longer fulfill its obligations as described, creating a clear contradiction within the contract, and an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any agreement concerning a relationship can only be made in a definitive written agreement, executed on behalf of each party by an authorized representative .
perturbation.changed_text: Any agreement concerning a relationship can only be made in a definitive agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of "written" and "executed on behalf of each party by an authorized representative" creates ambiguity. It now suggests that verbal agreements or agreements executed by unauthorized individuals could be considered valid, conflicting with standard contract law principles and undermining the purpose of a formal agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof and supersedes a ll prior agreements between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof. No agreement modifying or waiving any provision of this Agreement shall be binding unless made in a writing that references this Agreement and is signed by the part ies. Facsimile or electronic signed copies shall have the same effect as originals.
perturbation.justification: The original text required agreements concerning relationships to be in a definitive written agreement executed by authorized representatives. The changed text removes "written" and "executed on behalf of each party by an authorized representative", creating ambiguity about whether verbal agreements or agreements executed by unauthorized individuals are valid. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that modifications must be 'in a writing that references this Agreement and is signed by the parties', thus establishing a strong contradiction because the changed text now allows for agreements that are not necessarily written or signed, while the contradicted text mandates written and signed agreements for any modification to be binding.
